summary: Install-time C2 backdoor with SSH key and AWS credential exfiltration
details: Package executes install.js via preinstall/install/postinstall lifecycle hooks and runs an IIFE on require() of index.js. At install time, install.js reads /root/.ssh/id_rsa, /etc/hosts, the full process.env, and a recursive filesystem listing, then PUTs base64-encoded contents to a hardcoded remote endpoint at 154.57.164.82:30843; it then polls the same endpoint in a 2-second loop, runs returned commands via child_process.execSync, and PUTs the output back — an interactive remote shell on the installer's machine. On require(), index.js performs additional reconnaissance: runs `aws sts get-caller-identity`, queries the EC2 instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 for IAM security credentials, runs `aws secretsmanager list-secrets`, and ships results to the same C2. A second module, recon.js, collects ~/.npmrc, Verdaccio htpasswd/config.yaml, cron, netstat, and container info and PUTs them to a second C2 at 154.57.164.76:30728. Execution is gated to hosts whose hostname matches the 12-hex-char Docker container ID pattern and bails out when known scanner markers are present (npm_package_resolved containing 'supplysec', INIT_CWD=='/home/node', NODE_OPTIONS containing 'aspect-node') — deliberate anti-analysis evasion. A shipped publish-and-arm.sh automates re-publishing the package and arming remote module slots for a dependency-confusion campaign against a Verdaccio-fronted private registry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
